steps.title: Find the slope
steps.content:
From the graph, the line passes through $(-12,0)$ and $(0,-11)$. Use these points to find the slope:
$$m=\frac{-11-0}{0-(-12)}=-\frac{11}{12}$$

steps.title: Write the line equation
steps.content:
The $y$-intercept is $-11$, so the equation of the line is
$$y=-\frac{11}{12}x-11$$

steps.title: Substitute the y-value
steps.content:
The point $(v,-5)$ lies on the line, so substitute $y=-5$:
$$-5=-\frac{11}{12}v-11$$
$$6=-\frac{11}{12}v$$
$$v=-\frac{72}{11}$$
The answer is A.
